module.title: SAP TM Organizational Structure & Master Data

topic.title: SAP TM Organization Management & Planning Responsibility

learningObjectives.title: Understand organization management
learningObjectives.description: Explain how planning responsibilities are distributed across TM organizations and planners.

learningObjectives.title: Understand planning responsibility criteria
learningObjectives.description: Identify assignment criteria such as region, plant, shipping point and transportation zone.

learningObjectives.title: Differentiate responsibility and authorization
learningObjectives.description: Clarify who plans transportation versus what users can access.

sections.content: Organization Management in SAP TM determines how transportation activities are distributed among planners and business units. It ensures that transportation requests are assigned to the correct planning organization, planning group and planner.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Organization Management controls planning responsibility, authorization and workload distribution across transportation planners.

Planning Responsibility determines which planner or planning organization is responsible for a transportation request. Assignment can be based on geography, transportation zone, shipping point, customer, plant or business rules.

A global manufacturing company has planning teams in India, Europe and North America. Freight Units generated for Indian customers are automatically assigned to the India Planning Organization, while European shipments are routed to the Europe Planning Organization.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A logistics company serving 18 countries configured planning responsibility based on Transportation Zones. Every Freight Unit was automatically assigned to the appropriate regional planning team without manual intervention.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Interviewers often ask how SAP TM automatically determines the responsible planner. Explain Planning Responsibility, Planning Organizations, Planning Groups and Rule-Based Assignment.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Planning Responsibility is not the same as Authorization. Responsibility determines who plans transportation, while Authorization determines what a user is allowed to access.

items: Assignments can be based on region, plant, customer or transportation zone.
Planning Organizations and Planning Groups work together.
Responsibility and Authorization are different concepts.
